channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Trees continue absorbing carbon after growth stops
item.description: Oak trees keep taking in carbon dioxide even after their annual growth ends, showing photosynthesis and wood production are not tightly linked.

item.title: Scientists resolve 150-year-old mystery about gallium
item.description: Researchers have overturned long-held beliefs about gallium by showing its atomic bonds reform at high temperatures, challenging decades of understanding.

item.title: Vitamin A discovery reshapes understanding of vision development
item.description: A surprising finding alters scientific views on how sharp central vision forms before birth, challenging previous assumptions about cone cell migration.

item.title: Ancient quasars challenge early universe theories
item.description: Astronomers discovered 31 of the oldest known quasars, including the two earliest ever seen, shining from a time just after the Big Bang.
